one_line_ask: Take six already-shot raw overhead flat-lay apparel photos, straighten and color-true each, cut every garment cleanly off its cluttered surface, place them all onto one consistent flat mid-gray backdrop, and export a high-res + web-ready square marketplace pack.
brief: Loomhearth Supply Co., a men's everyday-basics label refreshing its eBay and marketplace storefront, needs a clean, consistent flat-lay listing pack built from photos already shot in-studio. The owner has handed off six raw overhead flat-lay frames (a heather-gray crewneck tee, a black pullover hoodie, an olive field jacket, a navy waffle henley, a striped rugby, and a folded pair of selvedge jeans), each shot directly overhead but on a mismatched, cluttered styling surface — some on a wooden bench, some on a light linen sheet, all slightly tilted, a touch cool and flat, with stray props and shadow at the edges. Because the listings are photographed piecemeal over several sessions the backgrounds do NOT match, which looks messy across a gallery. The job: (1) straighten every frame so the garment sits square to the crop; (2) true up each garment's color and exposure so fabric reads crisp and true-to-life (correct the cool cast, lift flat exposure, gently push vibrance — subtle post only, no heavy stylizing); (3) lift each garment cleanly off its surface — select the garment as subject, then remove the background so each piece is an isolated transparent PNG with clean edges (no stray
